questionText: Provide the amount and proportion of your financial metrics from the reporting year that are vulnerable to the substantive effects of environmental risks.
questionKo: 보고연도의 재무지표 중 환경 리스크의 중대한 영향에 취약한 ( vulnerable) 금액과 비율을 입력하십시오 .
sectorTags: Financial services, Oil & Gas, Coal, General, Aviation, Capital goods, Transport OEMS - EPM, Chemicals, Metals & mining, Transport services, Steel, Agricultural commodities, Transport OEMS, Energy utilities & power generators, Cement, Food, beverage & tobacco, Paper & forestry, Real estate, Construction
simpleHelp: This question provides investors and data users with more transparency over the vulnerability of an organization to the substantive effects of environmental risks. Quantifying vulnerability can provide an understanding of the degree to which the organization stands to be affected by their anticipated risks. This can inform how and where the organization responds to environmental risk within their financial planning and strategy.

columns.text: Amount of financial metric vulnerable to transition risks for this environmental issue (unit currency as selected in 1.2)
columns.textKo: Amount of financial metric vulnerable to transition risks for this environmental issue (unit currency as 선택 in 1.2)

columns.text: % of total financial metric vulnerable to transition risks for this environmental issue
columns.textKo: % of total financial metric vulnerable to transition risks for this environmental issue

columns.text: Amount of financial metric vulnerable to physical risks for this environmental issue (unit currency as selected in 1.2)
columns.textKo: Amount of financial metric vulnerable to physical risks for this environmental issue (unit currency as 선택 in 1.2)

columns.text: % of total financial metric vulnerable to physical risks for this environmental issue
columns.textKo: % of total financial metric vulnerable to physical risks for this environmental issue

columns.text: Amount of CAPEX in the reporting year deployed towards risks related to this environmental issue
columns.textKo: Amount of 자본지출(CAPEX) in the 보고연도 deployed towards risks related to this environmental issue

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select any of the "Yes,…" options in response to column 1 "Environmental risks identified" in the "Climate change", "Forests", or "Water" row of 3.1.
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 you select 하나 이상 의 "Yes,…" options in response to 열 1 "Environmental risks identified" in the "기후변화", "Forests", 또는 "수자원" 행 of 3.1.

guidance.text:
General 
While question 3.1.1 asks for the financial effects of risks identified per specific risk and time horizon, this question enables investors and data users to understand the amount and proportion of your organization's current financial metrics that are vulnerable to financial effects of all environmental risks identified, regardless of when they may occur. This gives context to an organization's risk profile and helps to put the effects of risks in perspective.
Options in column 1 "Environmental issue" are dependent on selections in column 1 "Environmental risks identified" of 3.1.
You should report at least one row for each environmental issue presented in column 1 "Environmental issue", based on the financial metric in column 2 "Financial metric" most relevant to your business. You may choose to add rows to report based on additional financial metrics.
As per the TCFD and TNFD LEAP guidance , "vulnerability encompasses a variety of concepts and elements, including sensitivity or susceptibility to harm and lack of capacity to cope and adapt" (see Explanation of Terms for definition of vulnerability). For organizations following the LEAP approach, vulnerability is determined in the Assess phase, considering the likelihood of the risk arising and the organization's ability to mitigate the risk.
Environmental risks to which your organization is vulnerable may relate to one or more environmental issues. For example, if you have assessed a particular asset to be vulnerable to climate-change induced flooding risk, you may include the asset in the calculation of vulnerability to both climate and water-related risks.
Consider the amount and proportion of the financial metric vulnerable to risks which are anticipated to have a substantive effect on the organization in any time horizon. For example, all assets vulnerable to physical risks in any of the short, medium, or long-term time horizons should be included when calculating the option selected in column 5 "Amount of financial metric vulnerable to physical risks for this environmental issue (unit currency as selected in 1.2)".
Financial metric (column 2) 
Select the financial metric for which you have assessed your organization's vulnerability to risks related to the environmental issue in column 1.
If reporting multiple financial metrics to indicate your organization's vulnerability to risks for each environmental issue, you may add rows to report additional financial metrics.
You should report the metrics that are most relevant to your business activities.
% of total financial metric vulnerable to physical/transition risks for this environmental issue (column 4 & 6) 
Out of the total of the financial metric selected in column 2 "Financial metric", indicate the proportion of that metric considered vulnerable to risks relating to this environmental issue.
Calculate this number by dividing the value provided in column 3 "Amount of financial metric vulnerable to transition risks for this environmental issue (unit currency as selected in 1.2)" by the total value of the selected financial metric.
E.g. If "Climate change" is the environmental issue in column 1 "Environmental issue" and "Revenue" is the financial metric selected in column 2 "Financial metric", divide the value of your revenue vulnerable to climate-related risks by your total revenue.
Select the closest range to the calculated % and provide an explanation in column 8 "Explanation of financial figures".
Amount of CAPEX in the reporting year deployed towards risks related to this environmental issue (column 7) 
This column only appears if you select "CAPEX" in column 2 "Financial metric".
Indicate the total amount of capital expenditure in the reporting year that was spent towards risks relating to each environmental issue.
E.g. If you purchased a particular asset in the reporting year as a response to a climate-related risk, the capital expenditure associated with this purchase should be included in the figure reported in row "Climate change".
Explanation of financial figures (column 8) 
There are differing approaches to the calculation of vulnerability to risk. Describe the methodology used to derive the figures, including:
The approach employed for calculation; and
Any assumptions underlying the figure.
guidance.textKo: 일반 While 문항 3.1.1 asks 에 대한 financial effects of risks identified per specific risk 및 time horizon, this 문항 enables investors 및 데이터 사용자 to understand the amount 및 proportion of 귀사의 current financial metrics that are vulnerable to financial effects of all environmental risks identified, regardless of 경우 they may occur. This gives context to an 조직의 risk profile 및 helps to put the effects of risks in perspective. Options in 열 1 "Environmental issue" are dependent on selections in 열 1 "Environmental risks identified" of 3.1. You 해야 합니다 report 최소 one 행 for each environmental issue presented in 열 1 "Environmental issue", based on the financial metric in 열 2 "Financial metric" most relevant to your business. You may choose to add 행 to report based on additional financial metrics. As per the TCFD 및 TNFD LEAP guidance , "vulnerability encompasses a variety of concepts 및 elements, 포함 sensitivity 또는 susceptibility to harm 및 lack of capacity to cope 및 adapt" (see Explanation of Terms for definition of vulnerability). For organizations following the LEAP approach, vulnerability is determined in the Assess phase, considering the likelihood 의 risk arising 및 the 조직의 ability to mitigate the risk. Environmental risks to which 귀사 is vulnerable may relate to one 또는 more environmental issues. For example, 만약 you have assessed a particular asset to be vulnerable to climate-change induced flooding risk, you may include the asset in the calculation of vulnerability to both climate 및 수자원-related risks. Consider the amount 및 proportion 의 financial metric vulnerable to risks which are anticipated to have a substantive effect on the organization in 하나 이상 time horizon. For example, all assets vulnerable to physical risks in 하나 이상 의 short, medium, 또는 long-term time horizons 해야 합니다 be included 경우 calculating the option 선택 in 열 5 "Amount of financial metric vulnerable to physical risks for this environmental issue (unit currency as 선택 in 1.2)". Financial metric (열 2) Select the financial metric for which you have assessed 귀사의 vulnerability to risks related to the environmental issue in 열 1. 만약 reporting multiple financial metrics to indicate 귀사의 vulnerability to risks for each environmental issue, you may add 행 to report additional financial metrics. You 해야 합니다 report the metrics that are most relevant to your business activities. % of total financial metric vulnerable to physical/transition risks for this environmental issue (열 4 & 6) Out 의 total 의 financial metric 선택 in 열 2 "Financial metric", indicate the proportion of that metric considered vulnerable to risks relating to this environmental issue. Calculate this number by dividing the value 입력 in 열 3 "Amount of financial metric vulnerable to transition risks for this environmental issue (unit currency as 선택 in 1.2)" by the total value 의 선택 financial metric. E.g. 만약 "기후변화" is the environmental issue in 열 1 "Environmental issue" 및 "매출" is the financial metric 선택 in 열 2 "Financial metric", divide the value of your 매출 vulnerable to climate-related risks by your total 매출. Select the closest range to the calculated % 및 provide an explanation in 열 8 "Explanation of financial figures". Amount of 자본지출(자본지출(CAPEX)) in the 보고연도 deployed towards risks related to this environmental issue (열 7) This 열 only appears 만약 you select "자본지출(자본지출(CAPEX))" in 열 2 "Financial metric". Indicate the total amount of 자본지출 in the 보고연도 that was spent towards risks relating to each environmental issue. E.g. 만약 you purchased a particular asset in the 보고연도 as a response to a climate-related risk, the 자본지출 associated with this purchase 해야 합니다 be included in the figure 보고 in 행 "기후변화". Explanation of financial figures (열 8) There are differing approaches to the calculation of vulnerability to risk. 설명 the 방법론 used to derive the figures, 포함: The approach employed for calculation; 및 하나 이상 assumptions underlying the figure.

guidance.text:
The organization discloses organization-wide information, including:
Amount and proportion of total annual revenue vulnerable to 1) physical risks and 2) transition risks, 
Amount and proportion of assets vulnerable to 1) physical risks and 2) transition risks, 
Amount of capital expenditure deployed towards risks.
guidance.textKo: The organization discloses organization-wide information, 포함: Amount 및 proportion of total 연간 매출 vulnerable to 1) physical risks 및 2) transition risks, Amount 및 proportion of assets vulnerable to 1) physical risks 및 2) transition risks, Amount of 자본지출 deployed towards risks.

guidance.text:
Assets: entities functioning as stores of value and over which ownership rights are enforced by institutional units, individually or collectively, and from which economic benefits may be derived by their owners by holding them, or using them, over a period of time (the economic benefits consist of primary incomes derived from the use of the asset and the value, including possible holding gains/losses, that could be realized by disposing of the asset or terminating it). 
Capital expenditure: a measure of the value of purchases of fixed assets such as property, buildings, an industrial plant, technology, or equipment. Put differently, CapEx is any type of expense that an organization capitalizes, or shows on its balance sheet as an investment, rather than on its income statement as an expenditure. 
Liabilities: an obligation which requires one unit (the debtor) to make a payment or a series of payments to the other unit (the creditor) in certain circumstances specified in a contract between them. 
Revenue: gross income arising from the operations of an organization over a period of time. 
Vulnerability : the propensity or predisposition to be adversely affected. Vulnerability encompasses a variety of concepts and elements, including sensitivity or susceptibility to harm and lack of capacity to cope and adapt ( Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change , 2022).
guidance.textKo: Assets: entities functioning as stores of value 및 over which ownership rights are enforced by institutional units, individually 또는 collectively, 및 from which economic benefits may be derived by their owners by holding them, 또는 using them, over a period of time (the economic benefits consist of primary incomes derived from the use 의 asset 및 the value, 포함 possible holding gains/losses, that could be realized by disposing 의 asset 또는 terminating it). 자본지출: a measure 의 value of purchases of fixed assets such as property, buildings, an industrial plant, technology, 또는 equipment. Put differently, 자본지출(자본지출(CAPEX)) is 하나 이상 type of expense that an organization capitalizes, 또는 shows on its balance sheet as an investment, rather than on its income statement as an expenditure. Liabilities: an obligation which requires one unit (the debtor) to make a payment 또는 a series of payments to the other unit (the creditor) in certain circumstances specified in a contract between them. 매출: gross income arising from the operations of an organization over a period of time. Vulnerability : the propensity 또는 predisposition to be adversely affected. Vulnerability encompasses a variety of concepts 및 elements, 포함 sensitivity 또는 susceptibility to harm 및 lack of capacity to cope 및 adapt ( Intergovernmental Panel on 기후변화 , 2022).

criteria.criteria:
ROUTE A)
For rows where 'Climate change' is selected in column 'Environmental issue':
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of rows disclosed. Partially completed rows will not receive full points.
A maximum of 7/7 points is available for this route.
OR
NOT APPLICABLE ROUTE)
'Climate change' NOT selected in column 'Environmental issue' in any row - 0/0 points
A maximum of 0/0 points is available for this route.
criteria.criteriaKo: 경로 A) For 행 여기서 '기후변화' is 선택 in 열 'Environmental issue': 점수가 부여됩니다 per 완성된 칸(셀) in proportion to the number of 행 공개. 부분적으로 완성된 행은 최고점을 받을 수 없습니다. 이 경로에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 7/7점입니다. 또는 NOT APPLICABLE 경로) '기후변화' NOT 선택 in 열 'Environmental issue' in 하나 이상 행 - 0/0 점 이 경로에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 0/0점입니다.

sectorTagsKo: 금융 서비스, 석유 및 가스, 석탄, 일반, 항공, 자본재, 운송 OEM - EPM, 화학, 금속 및 광업, 운송 서비스, 철강, 농산물 원자재, 운송 OEM, 에너지 유틸리티 및 발전, 시멘트, Food, beverage & tobacco, 제지 및 임업, 부동산, 건설
simpleHelpKo: This 문항 provides investors 및 데이터 사용자 with more transparency over the vulnerability of an organization to the substantive effects of environmental risks. Quantifying vulnerability can provide an understanding 의 degree to which the organization stands to be affected by their anticipated risks. This can inform how 및 여기서 the organization responds to environmental risk within their 재무계획 및 strategy.
